text.p: parts of West Coast, Eastern Eyre Peninsula and North West Pastoral districts.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while storms are nearby.

warning-info.text: The next warning is due to be issued by 8:35 pm.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

Weather Situation: A cloud band with embedded thunderstorms is moving from east of ceduna, through the central parts of the Eyre Peninsula this afternoon and into the evening, generating heavier rainfalls.

Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Wudinna, Tarcoola and Kimba.

hazard.text: West Coast, Eastern Eyre Peninsula and North West Pastoral

text.p: Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Wudinna, Tarcoola and Kimba.
